title: The South English Ministry and Passion (Laud Misc. 108 Fragment)
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00148. It encodes Carl Horstmann's 1873 title page and printed pp. 29-69: his 901-line transcription of the incomplete Laud Misc. 108 witness of the anonymous 3,048-line South English Ministry and Passion, with 204 editorial footnotes and the closing manuscript note. One footnote to relative line 761 also transcribes a 58-line passage of the distinct South English Legendary Life of St Mary Magdalene from Laud ff. 191r-v (Horstmann pp. 61-62; PDF pp. 73-74). Horstmann's German introduction on pp. 1-28 is not encoded. The target Laud fragment occupies ff. 1r-10v and represents work lines 874-2047, with about 272 lines lost between ff. 1 and 2; the principal fuller witness is St John's College Cambridge MS B.6, ff. 35r-69v. Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.25 preserves a non-overlapping Pater Noster extract that begins around mid-page on f. 270r/canvas 571 after the terminal St Silvester text and continues only through the upper portion of f. 270v/canvas 572, but DIMEV's reported 52-line extent conflicts with its inclusive work-line range 427-472. Complete public facsimiles of the entire Laud and Trinity codices are cached and mapped to the target and supporting portions. No public full-image surrogate of St John's B.6 was located, so its reading-room and enquiry route is recorded. The target fragment, quoted related work, abstract work, manuscript witnesses, nineteenth-century edition, later full edition, digital encodings, facsimiles, related Southern Passion revision, and repository artifact remain distinct.
review_notes: CME00148 is not cataloged as a generic Life of Jesus or as the separate Infancy of Jesus. Horstmann's Leben Jesu is an editorial title for the Laud fragment of The South English Ministry and Passion.
St John's B.6 is a parallel fuller witness, not an ancestor of Horstmann's target text. Trinity R.3.25 preserves a collateral extract outside the portion surviving in Laud.
The 58 Middle English lines nested in the note to target line 761 are a passage-scoped quotation of a different work from the same codex, not additional lines of the Ministry and Passion.
The South English Ministry and Passion is earlier than and was revised into the Southern Passion; the direction reverses an older view corrected by Pickering in 1984.
Witness dialect localizations and manuscript production places are not transferred to the abstract work's place of composition.

entities.label: The South English Ministry and Passion
alternate_labels: The Ministry and Passion of Christ
entities.description: Anonymous Middle English temporale narrative comprising 3,048 lines in septenary rhyming couplets, probably composed as a sequel to The Nativity of Mary and Christ and subsequently revised into the Southern Passion.

date_statements.label: composition
date_statements.notes: Liszka applies the pre-1290 terminus of the Southern Passion to its source, the Ministry and Passion; this is a terminus ante quem, not an exact date.

entities.label: The Southern Passion
entities.description: A later Middle English revision of the Ministry and Passion that introduces closer Vulgate translation and additional expository material.

entities.label: Life of St Mary Magdalene (South English Legendary version)
alternate_labels: Life of St. Mary Magdalene according to some MSS of the South English Legendary
entities.description: The distinct Middle English saint's life cataloged as DIMEV 4928 (NIMEV 3159). Horstmann quotes 58 lines beginning at his line 82 in the apparatus to Ministry and Passion relative line 761.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: The direct source of Horstmann's fragment: ff. 1r-10v preserve 901 lines corresponding to work lines 874-2047, with about 272 lines missing between ff. 1 and 2 and both outer boundaries incomplete.

date_statements.label: target hand and codex core
date_statements.value: c. 1300; late thirteenth or early fourteenth century
date_statements.notes: The catalog's broad machine range is 1290-1400; the digital object supplies 1301-1325, and Hand A is described as late thirteenth or early fourteenth century.

physical_description.extent: 240 or 243 leaves in the present composite codex
notes: Hand A wrote ff. 1-200; the first quire has uncertain structure and multiple physical losses.

entities.label: St John's College Cambridge MS B.6 (James 28)
entities.description: The principal fuller witness of the Ministry and Passion at ff. 35r-69v, following The Nativity of Mary and Christ at ff. 25r-35r; it is the sole witness for most of the work.

date_statements.label: manuscript date attributed to M. R. James
date_statements.notes: James prints the date with a question mark; it is retained as an attributed alternative rather than merged with Pickering's date into an invented normalized interval.

notes: 44 lines to a page; modern binding.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.25 (James 605)
entities.description: A fifteenth-century South English Legendary manuscript whose Pater Noster extract begins around mid-page on f. 270r/canvas 571 after the terminal St Silvester text and continues only through the upper portion of f. 270v/canvas 572. DIMEV maps it to Ministry and Passion lines 427-472 and calls it 52 lines although that inclusive range contains 46 line positions. The lower portion of f. 270v opens the next catalog item, 'Judas was a luþer bryd.' The extract does not overlap the Laud fragment.

notes: 46-48 lines to a page.

subject.description: Encoded title page, poem, page markers, 204 footnotes including the 58-line Magdalene quotation, and closing note.

subject.description: The 901-line Ministry and Passion reading text on printed pp. 29-69 (PDF pp. 41-81), excluding the separate Magdalene quotation nested in the footnote to relative line 761.

object.description: The surviving Ministry and Passion fragment.

scope.notes: Horstmann states that the target text is reproduced closely from the manuscript, with supplied punctuation, capitalized proper names, and expanded abbreviations. The separate apparatus transcription from ff. 191r-v has its own scoped edge.

subject.description: The 58-line Life of St Mary Magdalene quotation in Horstmann's footnote to Ministry and Passion relative line 761.

object.description: The quoted passage of the Life of St Mary Magdalene.

scope.notes: The XML serializes all 58 quoted L elements inside one NOTE before the PB for printed p. 62, although the quotation continues onto p. 62 in the source edition. This apparatus quotation is supporting lineage, not part of the Ministry and Passion target text.

subject.description: Complete Laud witness of the Life of St Mary Magdalene at ff. 190r-197r; the CME00148 apparatus quotes only ff. 191r-v.

object.description: DIMEV 4928, the Life of St Mary Magdalene according to some South English Legendary manuscripts.

subject.description: Pater Noster extract beginning around mid-page on f. 270r/canvas 571 after the terminal St Silvester text and continuing only through the upper portion of f. 270v/canvas 572; DIMEV reports 52 lines.

object.description: DIMEV maps the extract to work lines 427-472, an inclusive span of only 46 line positions.

scope.notes: The extract does not overlap the Laud fragment at work lines 874-2047. rights.basis: The work was composed in the thirteenth century and its author is unknown.

rights.component: Anonymous medieval Life of St Mary Magdalene text quoted in the apparatus
rights.basis: DIMEV catalogs the anonymous Middle English work in medieval manuscript witnesses; this underlying historical text is distinct from Horstmann's editorial transcription and modern manuscript photographs.

rights.component: Kyvero Press repository software under the pinned CME root LICENSE
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository root LICENSE uses the Unlicense public-domain dedication and fallback license for software.
notes: The license wording says software. Its applicability to inherited textual data, Horstmann's edition, or manuscript images is not assumed.

rights.component: U-M CME digital item and encoded historical text as presented
rights.basis: The current U-M CME collection describes its source editions and collection materials as public domain in the United States and applies the No Copyright - United States statement. The exact item and collection interfaces returned an automated HTTP 403 challenge during this audit, so this collection-level application is recorded at medium confidence rather than inferred from the unrelated Internet Archive scan.
First published in 1873, well before the current United States public-domain cutoff.
notes: No worldwide conclusion is based on an unverified death date for Horstmann.

rights.component: Google-derived scan distributed by Internet Archive
rights.basis: The Internet Archive metadata applies the Public Domain Mark 1.0.
The exact IIIF manifest attribution applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to the photographs.
No public image sequence or item-level reuse license was located; terms must be obtained with any supplied images.

rights.component: Pickering's 1984 introduction, critical text, apparatus, glossary, and layout
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The modern scholarly edition was published in 1984 and no open license was located; controlled digital lending is access, not redistribution permission.
Leeds makes the article publicly downloadable but does not display an open reuse license and identifies University of Leeds rights in the images.

rights.component: Original material in the open-access scholarly volume
rights.basis: The volume states that original material is distributed under CC BY-NC 4.0; third-party diagrams, illustrations, photographs, and text samples may require separate permission.

rights.component: Complete Trinity College Cambridge manuscript image sequence
rights.basis: The manuscript catalog and exact IIIF manifest apply CC BY-NC 4.0 to the digital work.
The Zenodo record applies CC BY 4.0 to the dataset.

editorial_practices.representation: Title page plus printed pp. 29-69 only
editorial_practices.description: The encoding retains the edition title page, the 901-line fragment, 204 footnotes, page breaks, and closing manuscript note, but omits Horstmann's German introduction on pp. 1-28 and the corrigenda.
editorial_practices.scope: Entire U-M encoding and repository XML

editorial_practices.description: Horstmann explicitly says that the manuscript's wholly absent punctuation was added.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English fragment on printed pp. 29-69

editorial_practices.description: Horstmann explicitly says that proper names were consistently given a capital initial.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English fragment on printed pp. 29-69

editorial_practices.description: Horstmann explicitly says that the few abbreviations were expanded; the statement does not claim that every expansion is typographically distinguished.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English fragment on printed pp. 29-69
notes: The complete Laud facsimile permits folio-level comparison of these interventions.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00148.xml, pinned CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD; IDG/BIBNO/VID; TEXT/@LANG; FRONT title page; BODY/DIV1/LG; footnote to relative line 761; PB 61-62; L; NOTE; TAILNOTE
evidence.summary: Establishes repository identity, U-M identifiers, source title page, exact encoded selection, page sequence, text boundaries, apparatus, the 58-line Life of St Mary Magdalene quotation nested in the note to target line 761, and the closing manuscript note.

evidence.citation: Programmatic and manual inspection of CME00148.xml.
evidence.locator: 2 DIV1; 42 PB; 1 LG; 961 total L elements; 903 direct poem L elements including two dotted boundary rows; 58 L elements in the NOTE attached to relative line 761; 204 NOTE; 12 P; 1 TAILNOTE; complete quotation serialized before PB N=62
evidence.summary: Confirms 901 verbal target-work lines plus two dotted boundary rows and identifies all 58 nested L elements as one coherent quotation from the Life of St Mary Magdalene rather than target-work extent. It also confirms seven title-page paragraphs, printed pp. 29-69 in the body, and one closing note, and shows that the quotation's p. 62 continuation precedes the XML PB for p. 62.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00148.
evidence.locator: canonical item identifier and full-text presentation
evidence.summary: Confirms the canonical U-M item route corresponding to the repository XML.
notes: Automated curl access returned an HTTP 403 challenge on the review date; repository XML and indexed item identity provide the content evidence.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page.
evidence.locator: Collection description and Rights and Permissions
evidence.summary: Official collection-level evidence that U-M presents CME source editions and collection materials as public domain in the United States and applies No Copyright - United States.
notes: Both the collection and exact-item routes returned an HTTP 403 Cloudflare challenge to the anonymous scripted check on the review date; this evidence records the collection statement rather than using the unrelated Internet Archive scan to support U-M rights.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: complete file

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, Leben Jesu, ein Fragment, und Kindheit Jesu, I. Theil: Leben Jesu (Münster, 1873).
evidence.locator: title page = PDF p. 11; printed pp. 1-3 = PDF pp. 13-15; printed p. 28 = PDF p. 40; target text printed pp. 29-69 = PDF pp. 41-81; Magdalene quotation printed pp. 61-62 = PDF pp. 73-74
evidence.summary: Names Laud 108 as the target source, identifies former shelfmarks Laud K 60 and Laud C 73, describes the 901-line incomplete fragment at the manuscript's beginning, prints it on pp. 29-69, and states that punctuation was supplied, proper names capitalized, and the few abbreviations expanded. The note to relative line 761 explicitly introduces and prints 58 lines from the Life of St Mary Magdalene in Laud 108, f. 191, beginning at that work's line 82.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, LebenJesuHorstmann, complete Google-derived scan and metadata.
evidence.locator: 88 scans/PDF pages; title at n10/PDF p. 11; target text at n40-n80/PDF pp. 41-81; Magdalene quotation at n72-n73/PDF pp. 73-74
evidence.summary: Provides the complete 1873 source volume, exact PDF/OCR downloads, scan-to-PDF-page mapping, bibliographic metadata, and the provider's Public Domain Mark 1.0 assertion.

evidence.citation: Gemeinsame Normdatei, Horstmann, Carl, GND 1153444186.
evidence.locator: preferred name, birth year, occupations, biography
evidence.summary: Distinguishes the English philologist and editor born in 1851 from the same-named ophthalmologist; no death date is supplied, so worldwide edition copyright is not inferred from one.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Laud Misc. 108.
evidence.locator: MS_Laud_Misc_108-item1: Ministry and Passion ff. 1r-10v; St Mary Magdalene ff. 190r-197r; history, physical description, Hand A
evidence.summary: Identifies the target as The Ministry and Passion of Christ, DIMEV 4633; gives 901 surviving lines, incomplete boundaries at work lines 874 and 2047, approximately three leaves or 272 lines missing between ff. 1 and 2, Horstmann pp. 29-69, the later directing note on f. 10v, the codex date and origin, and physical structure. It separately identifies the Life of St Mary Magdalene at ff. 190r-197r as DIMEV 4928.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF manifest for MS Laud Misc. 108.
evidence.locator: 496 canvases; target canvases 9-30, including fragment canvases 11-12; Magdalene quotation source ff. 191r-v = canvases 397-398

The private bundle contains all 496 canvases at the exact effective width-only profile 1600, in JPEG format.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of St John's College, Cambridge (1913), 37-38.

evidence.summary: Describes B.6 as an 84-folio paper manuscript with 44 lines per page and queried late-fourteenth-century date, and records its complete temporale and Life of the Virgin and Christ at ff. 25-69v.

evidence.citation: Targeted search for a public complete digital surrogate of St John's College Cambridge MS B.6 (James 28).
evidence.locator: repository site, general web search, IIIF discovery, DIMEV and catalog references
evidence.summary: No public full-manuscript image sequence, IIIF manifest, or posted reproduction price was located. This supports a no-public-copy finding, not a claim that the manuscript has never been digitized internally.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, southenglishmini0000unse, controlled-access scan metadata for Oliver S. Pickering, The South English Ministry and Passion (1984); corroborated by Folger catalog record 44438.
evidence.locator: metadata, access-restricted-item=true, 262 images, private unencrypted PDF and OCR
evidence.summary: Confirms the 256-page 1984 full edition, ISBN 3-533-03490-9, St John's B.6 source statement, and controlled rather than openly downloadable access.

evidence.citation: Oliver S. Pickering, 'The Southern Passion and the Ministry and Passion: The Work of a Middle English Reviser,' Leeds Studies in English, n.s. 15 (1984): 33-56.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 33-36 = PDF pp. 2-5; printed pp. 51-56 = PDF pp. 20-25; St John's B.6 ff. 35r-69v, Laud ff. 1r-10v, Trinity R.3.25 f. 270r-v
evidence.summary: Establishes that the Southern Passion is a revision of the Ministry and Passion, calls B.6 early fifteenth century and the sole witness for most of MP, identifies all three witnesses and exact work locations, and describes preparation of the first full edition. The 25-page provider PDF text-extracts and separates completely; qpdf reports recoverable linearization hint-table/shared-object-list warnings, so the exact bytes were retained without repair.

evidence.citation: Kimberly K. Bell and Julie Nelson Couch, eds., The Texts and Contexts of Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 108 (Brill, 2011).
evidence.locator: Edwards printed pp. 21-30 = PDF pp. 44-53; Liszka printed pp. 31-50 = PDF pp. 54-73, especially printed pp. 43-44 = PDF pp. 66-67; Kline printed pp. 137-157 = PDF pp. 160-180, especially printed pp. 141-143 = PDF pp. 164-166
evidence.summary: Provides current codicological and interpretive context for Laud 108; Liszka applies a pre-1290 terminus to MP because Southern Passion derives from it, and Kline reports Pickering's view that MP was probably a sequel to The Nativity of Mary and Christ and the earliest Passion narrative in the temporale group. The volume states CC BY-NC 4.0 for original material with a third-party-material caveat. The 365-page provider PDF text-extracts and separates completely; qpdf reports recoverable linearization hint-table/shared-object offset and page-list warnings, so the exact bytes were retained without repair.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, James Catalogue of Western Manuscripts, R.3.25.
evidence.locator: main details; contents item 115 St Silvester; item 116 Pater Noster at f. 270; item 117 at f. 270v; item 118 at f. 272; IIIF route; license
evidence.summary: Supplies the fifteenth-century date, parchment support, 275-folio extent, and the catalog sequence placing the Pater Noster (item 116) after St Silvester (item 115) and before the next item opening on f. 270v. It also supplies the complete IIIF route and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF manifest for R.3.25.
evidence.locator: 586 canvases; canvas 571 = f. 270r, with terminal St Silvester above the Pater Noster beginning around mid-page; upper canvas 572 = f. 270v and terminal Pater Noster portion; lower canvas 572 opens item 117
evidence.summary: Supplies a complete 586-canvas codex sequence with exact source inventory and maps the collateral Pater Noster extract from around mid-page on canvas 571, after the terminal St Silvester text, through only the upper portion of canvas 572. It applies CC BY-NC 4.0. All 586 cached images use the effective width-only profile 1600, in JPEG format.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, v1.0 dataset, records 4633 (NIMEV 2931.55) and 4928 (NIMEV 3159).
evidence.locator: Records.xml record-4633 with witnesses 4633-1, 4633-2, and 4633-5; record-4928 with witness 4928-1; Manuscripts.xml BodLauMis108, CamStJohn28, CamTCC605; Bibliography.xml Horstmann1873a and Pickering1984
evidence.summary: Defines the temporale narrative as 3,048 lines arranged in couplets, links it as a continuation of record 6380, gives the three witnesses and exact folios, reports the Trinity extract as 52 lines while mapping it to lines 427-472, identifies Horstmann pp. 29-69 and Pickering's full edition, and localizes the relevant scribal dialects. Record 4928 identifies the distinct Life of St Mary Magdalene and its Laud witness at ff. 190r-197r. Zenodo supplies CC BY 4.0.
notes: The exact cached Zenodo file URL returned HTTP 403 during one anonymous scripted audit and HTTP 200 during a later check; the verified cache, DOI, and live records preserve the public-release evidence.

open_questions.question: Can St John's College supply or identify a complete digital facsimile of MS B.6 (28), and what fees and reuse terms would apply?
next_steps: Email special.collections@joh.cam.ac.uk with shelfmark B.6 (28), requesting the whole manuscript if feasible and at minimum ff. 25r-69v.
Ask separately for image reuse and repository-publication terms.

open_questions.question: How should the official work-line boundaries 874-2047, the 272-line internal loss, and the reported 901 surviving lines be reconciled arithmetically?
next_steps: Consult Pickering's 1984 edition pp. 8-10 and its lineation table through controlled access.
Map every surviving Laud line against Pickering's full-work line numbers.
notes: Inclusive arithmetic across 874-2047 minus 272 yields 902 rather than 901, so at least one boundary or loss count is approximate or exclusive.

open_questions.question: Why does DIMEV call the Trinity R.3.25 Pater Noster extract 52 lines while mapping it to Ministry and Passion lines 427-472, an inclusive range of 46 positions?
next_steps: Consult Pickering's full 1984 edition and lineation table through controlled access.

Transcribe and align the passage from around mid-page on f. 270r/canvas 571, after the terminal St Silvester text, through the upper portion of f. 270v/canvas 572, stopping before the opening of contents item 117.
notes: Canvas 571 is shared with the terminal St Silvester text above item 116; canvas 572 is shared with item 117, so only its upper portion completes the extract before the next text begins below a large red initial.

open_questions.question: Should St John's B.6 be dated to the queried late fourteenth century or the early fifteenth century?
next_steps: Consult Pickering's full 1984 manuscript description and any newer St John's cataloging or paleographical study.
